decisions.title: Scale data-batcher to multiple pods in production
decisions.context: ENG-9018 reveals 3× memory multiplier causing OOM. ENG-9050 shows dashboard prep for multi-pod. Need to decide on horizontal scaling strategy vs optimization.

arguments.for: Immediate relief from OOM issues, better fault tolerance, dashboards already being prepped
arguments.against: May mask underlying efficiency problem, increases operational complexity, cost implications

decisions.title: Prioritize Fresh Data Reads (ENG-8228) for Explore V2 GA
decisions.context: Fresh data from MemTable is foundational for real-time Explore V2. Success criteria being defined (ENG-8225). Directly supports 'Nail the Core' pillar and Decisions Captured metric.

arguments.for: Unlocks real-time decision-making, differentiator vs competitors, high customer demand
arguments.against: Complex implementation, may delay other Explore V2 features, requires additional infrastructure work

decisions.title: Consolidate alerting channels for DLv3 components
decisions.context: ENG-9058 and ENG-9052 show alerts routing to wrong channels. Fragmented alerting reduces response time and creates confusion. Need unified strategy.
decisions.blockedBy: Need agreement on channel ownership and escalation paths across teams

arguments.for: Faster incident response, clearer ownership, reduced noise in eng-production-alerts
arguments.against: Requires coordination across multiple teams, potential for missed alerts during transition

decisions.title: Finalize Python SDK exception/logging patterns before expanding adoption
decisions.context: FD-102, FD-100, FD-101 all address foundational patterns. Python Rules is key 2026 product. Inconsistent patterns will create tech debt as adoption grows.

arguments.for: Prevents tech debt, better developer experience, easier debugging at scale
arguments.against: May slow current feature development, patterns might need revision based on real usage

decisions.title: Ship Annotations to Quick Actions (ENG-9060)
decisions.context: Moving from context menu to Quick Actions improves discoverability. Annotations support Signal>Evidence workflow. Context menu work (ENG-8816) may conflict with this direction.

arguments.for: Better UX, aligns with Canvas design system, higher feature adoption expected
arguments.against: Context menu interactions already in progress, may confuse users with location change

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of work hours) limiting deep work on Fresh Data architecture and Python SDK patterns. Consider batching stakeholder updates.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks scheduled this week - insufficient for architecture decisions pending
Three 1:1s today consuming prime morning hours - consider async updates for status discussions
Back-to-back meetings Tue-Thu blocking code review for critical DLv3 performance fixes
